content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 18, 1991

Joyce Goudie, Assistant Registrar of Voters
Stanislaus County Clerk-Recorder
912 11th Street, Room 100
Modesto, CA 95354

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-91-303

Dear Ms. Goudie:

     You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

     What are the campaign disclosure requirements for an
officeholder who has been appointed to an elective office?

CONCLUSION

     A person who is appointed to an elective office is defined in
the Act as an "elected officer." Elected officers whose salaries
are $100 or more per month are required to file semi-annual
campaign statements each year. Elected officers whose salaries
are less than $100 per month are required to file semi-annual
campaign statements only if they make or receive contributions or
make expenditures during the semi-annual period. Pre-election
campaign statements and additional special reports may also be
required when an elected officer is involved in an election.

FACTS

     Jim Jasper was first appointed to the Board of Directors of
the Central California Irrigation District in December of 1987.

___________________________

1/   Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Joyce Goudie
Page 2



At the last election, Mr. Jasper was reappointed when no opposing
candidate filed for the position. Since his first appointment, he
has filed one Candidate and Officeholder Campaign Statement-Short
Form (Form 470) which covered 1989.

Although you received verbal advice from the District earlier
this year that its directors receive compensation of $100 or more
per month, Paul R. Minasian, attorney for Mr. Jasper, has
indicated by letter dated June 21, 1991 (copy enclosed), that
directors of the Central California Irrigation District receive a
per diem rate of $50 per meeting. Mr. Minasian has also stated
that the Irrigation District generally has one regular meeting per
month, but if the regular meeting is adjourned, there may be a
second meeting.

ANALYSIS

Section 82020 provides that "a person appointed to fill a
vacant elective office is an elected officer." Section 84200
requires elected officers to file two semi-annual campaign
statements each calendar year. An exception is made for elected
officers whose salaries are less than $100 per month and who do
not make or receive contributions or make expenditures during the
semi-annual period.2/

Mr. Minasian indicates that the payments Mr. Jasper receives
are per diem payments based on meetings attended and should not be
considered salary. However, in In re Goodwin (1 FPPC 24, copy
enclosed), the Commission determined that the terms "compensation"
and "salary" are synonymous. Therefore, the compensation provided
special district directors in the form of per diem payments must
be regarded as salary within the meaning of the Act.

Whether Mr. Jasper has an obligation to file semi-annual
statements as an elected officer is determined by the amount of
compensation he becomes entitled to receive each month. The
average salary over the semi-annual period should be the basis for
determining whether an elected officer is within the exemption for
receiving salary of less than $100 a month. Hence, an elected
officer who ordinarily meets once a month, with compensation of
$50 per meeting, but infrequently meets twice in one month, would
not be required to file a semi-annual campaign statement unless he



2/        Another exception is provided for elected officers who will
receive contributions totaling less than $1,000 and who will make
expenditures totaling less than $1,000 during the entire calendar
year. These officeholders may reduce their filing obligations by
filing one Candidate and Officeholder Campaign Statement-Short
Form (Form 470) for the entire calendar year. (Section 84206,
Regulation 18406.)

Joyce Goudie
Page 3




or she receives contributions or makes expenditures as defined in
Sections 82015 and 82025, respectively, during the semi-annual
period.  (1 FPPC at 26.)

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [illegible]
                                        By: Wayne P. Imberi
                                            Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
cc:  Paul R. Minasian

sections.question: What are the campaign disclosure requirements for an officeholder who has been appointed to an elective office?
sections.conclusion: A person who is appointed to an elective office is defined in the Act as an "elected officer." Elected officers whose salaries are less than $100 per month are required to file semi-annual campaign statements only if they make or receive contributions or make expenditures during the semi-annual period. Pre-election campaign statements and additional special reports may also be required when an elected officer is involved in an election.
